notes: NEW this round (md section 18.3). src/sensing/segmentation.py:select_aligned_segments. naive (default) reproduces segment_regions() byte-for-byte (regression-confirmed: synthetic mode, single-burst radioml known baseline (0.625/48/64), multi-burst mode both ambiguous- and unambiguous-truth-match cases all unchanged). max-energy selects one highest-mean-power seg_len window per detected region via sliding search, never referencing true_burst_start/end (structural guarantee, not convention). Validated via a real 7-modulation comparison (md 18.4) with normalization held constant to isolate the effect: max-energy reaches 4/7 accuracy (matches the oracle upper bound exactly) and 7/7 prediction agreement with the embedded-ground-truth-slice oracle, vs naive's 2/7 accuracy and 3/7 agreement. One instability found and reported as required (not engineered around): for BPSK specifically, max-energy's segment-level capture ratio (0.586) was marginally below naive's (0.625) because the region itself only partially covered the true burst -- 'highest energy' is not always exactly 'maximum true-burst overlap' when real signal power varies sample-to-sample. Reproducibility confirmed bit-identical cross-process (2 independent processes, QAM16/snr18/idx0, max-energy).
